key_insight: CuCrZr’s nano-precipitation strengthening is not stable under fusion-relevant irradiation: it degrades by different mechanisms at low and high temperatures.
engineering_relevance: This threatens the long-term reliability of CuCrZr heat-sinks in fusion reactors and signals a need for new Cu-based alloys that retain strength under neutron exposure.
method: In situ TEM during heavy-ion irradiation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent precipitate degradation under both irradiation-temperature extremes, plus He/Kr defect formation and a modeled five-year transmutation shift toward Ni-Zr intermetallics.
watch_item: Ion-irradiation dose rates differ from reactor neutron conditions, so direct lifetime translation to actual fusion service needs caution.
paper_title: CuCrZr heat-sink irradiation performance reveals new challenges for thermonuclear fusion reactors
